title: SEDI Voter ID Credential
description: Demo credential asserting voter eligibility for the SEDI issuer holder verifier flow.

usageDisclaimer.d: Use of a valid, unexpired, and non-revoked SEDI Voter ID Credential does not by itself constitute a ballot, voter registration record, government-issued identity document, or independent grant of voting rights. The credential is a digital attestation of the voter eligibility attributes asserted by the issuer for the election identified in the credential.

issuerAuthority.d: The issuer represents that it is authorized to issue this credential for the stated jurisdiction and election, and that the credential is based on election records, eligibility records, or verification procedures available to the issuer at the time of issuance.

holderUse.d: The holder may present this credential only to demonstrate the attributes asserted in the credential. The holder must not alter, misrepresent, transfer, or present the credential after expiration, revocation, or loss of eligibility.

verifierReliance.d: A verifier may rely on this credential only after independently verifying the issuer identifier, schema identifier, credential status registry, cryptographic signatures, issuance status, revocation status, expiration time, and any jurisdiction-specific eligibility requirements.

privacyLimitation.d: Verifiers should request, display, store, and retain only the minimum credential information necessary for the stated election-administration purpose and must handle any personal information according to applicable privacy, election, public-records, and records-retention laws.

revocationAndExpiration.d: This credential is invalid after the expiration time stated in the credential or upon revocation in the credential status registry, whichever occurs first. Revocation or expiration does not by itself adjudicate voter eligibility outside the scope of this credential.

noAdditionalWarranty.d: Except for the issuer's express assertions contained in the credential, this credential is provided without additional warranties. The legal effect of the credential depends on applicable law, issuer authority, verifier policy, and the facts existing at the time of presentation.
